root_text.an4.233:1.1: “Cattārimāni, bhikkhave, kammāni mayā sayaṁ abhiññā sacchikatvā paveditāni.
root_text.an4.233:1.6: atthi, bhikkhave, kammaṁ akaṇhaasukkaṁ akaṇhaasukkavipākaṁ kammakkhayāya saṁvattati.
root_text.an4.233:2.2: Idha, bhikkhave, ekacco sabyābajjhaṁ kāyasaṅkhāraṁ abhisaṅkharoti, sabyābajjhaṁ vacīsaṅkhāraṁ abhisaṅkharoti, sabyābajjhaṁ manosaṅkhāraṁ abhisaṅkharoti.
root_text.an4.233:2.3: So sabyābajjhaṁ kāyasaṅkhāraṁ abhisaṅkharitvā, sabyābajjhaṁ vacīsaṅkhāraṁ abhisaṅkharitvā, sabyābajjhaṁ manosaṅkhāraṁ abhisaṅkharitvā sabyābajjhaṁ lokaṁ upapajjati.
root_text.an4.233:2.4: Tamenaṁ sabyābajjhaṁ lokaṁ upapannaṁ samānaṁ sabyābajjhā phassā phusanti.
root_text.an4.233:2.5: So sabyābajjhehi phassehi phuṭṭho samāno sabyābajjhaṁ vedanaṁ vediyati ekantadukkhaṁ, seyyathāpi sattā nerayikā.
root_text.an4.233:2.6: Idaṁ vuccati, bhikkhave, kammaṁ kaṇhaṁ kaṇhavipākaṁ.
root_text.an4.233:3.2: Idha, bhikkhave, ekacco abyābajjhaṁ kāyasaṅkhāraṁ abhisaṅkharoti, abyābajjhaṁ vacīsaṅkhāraṁ abhisaṅkharoti, abyābajjhaṁ manosaṅkhāraṁ abhisaṅkharoti.
root_text.an4.233:3.3: So abyābajjhaṁ kāyasaṅkhāraṁ abhisaṅkharitvā, abyābajjhaṁ vacīsaṅkhāraṁ abhisaṅkharitvā, abyābajjhaṁ manosaṅkhāraṁ abhisaṅkharitvā abyābajjhaṁ lokaṁ upapajjati.
root_text.an4.233:3.4: Tamenaṁ abyābajjhaṁ lokaṁ upapannaṁ samānaṁ abyābajjhā phassā phusanti.
root_text.an4.233:3.5: So abyābajjhehi phassehi phuṭṭho samāno abyābajjhaṁ vedanaṁ vediyati ekantasukhaṁ, seyyathāpi devā subhakiṇhā.
root_text.an4.233:3.6: Idaṁ vuccati, bhikkhave, kammaṁ sukkaṁ sukkavipākaṁ.
root_text.an4.233:4.2: Idha, bhikkhave, ekacco sabyābajjhampi abyābajjhampi kāyasaṅkhāraṁ abhisaṅkharoti, sabyābajjhampi abyābajjhampi vacīsaṅkhāraṁ abhisaṅkharoti, sabyābajjhampi abyābajjhampi manosaṅkhāraṁ abhisaṅkharoti.
root_text.an4.233:4.3: So sabyābajjhampi abyābajjhampi kāyasaṅkhāraṁ abhisaṅkharitvā, sabyābajjhampi abyābajjhampi vacīsaṅkhāraṁ abhisaṅkharitvā, sabyābajjhampi abyābajjhampi manosaṅkhāraṁ abhisaṅkharitvā sabyābajjhampi abyābajjhampi lokaṁ upapajjati.
root_text.an4.233:4.4: Tamenaṁ sabyābajjhampi abyābajjhampi lokaṁ upapannaṁ samānaṁ sabyābajjhāpi abyābajjhāpi phassā phusanti.
root_text.an4.233:4.5: So sabyābajjhehipi abyābajjhehipi phassehi phuṭṭho samāno sabyābajjhampi abyābajjhampi vedanaṁ vediyati vokiṇṇasukhadukkhaṁ, seyyathāpi manussā ekacce ca devā ekacce ca vinipātikā.
root_text.an4.233:4.6: Idaṁ vuccati, bhikkhave, kammaṁ kaṇhasukkaṁ kaṇhasukkavipākaṁ.
root_text.an4.233:5.1: Katamañca, bhikkhave, kammaṁ akaṇhaasukkaṁ akaṇhaasukkavipākaṁ kammakkhayāya saṁvattati?
root_text.an4.233:5.2: Tatra, bhikkhave, yamidaṁ kammaṁ kaṇhaṁ kaṇhavipākaṁ tassa pahānāya yā cetanā, yamidaṁ kammaṁ sukkaṁ sukkavipākaṁ tassa pahānāya yā cetanā, yamidaṁ kammaṁ kaṇhasukkaṁ kaṇhasukkavipākaṁ tassa pahānāya yā cetanā—
root_text.an4.233:5.3: idaṁ vuccati, bhikkhave, kammaṁ akaṇhaasukkaṁ akaṇhaasukkavipākaṁ kammakkhayāya saṁvattati.
root_text.an4.233:5.4: Imāni kho, bhikkhave, cattāri kammāni mayā sayaṁ abhiññā sacchikatvā paveditānī”ti.

translation_text.an4.233:1.1: “Mendicants, I declare these four kinds of deeds, having realized them with my own insight.
translation_text.an4.233:1.3: There are dark deeds with dark results;
translation_text.an4.233:1.5: dark and bright deeds with dark and bright results; and
translation_text.an4.233:1.6: neither dark nor bright deeds with neither dark nor bright results, which lead to the ending of deeds.
translation_text.an4.233:2.1: And what are dark deeds with dark results?
translation_text.an4.233:2.2: It’s when someone makes hurtful choices by way of body, speech, and mind.
translation_text.an4.233:2.3: Having made these choices, they’re reborn in a hurtful world,
translation_text.an4.233:2.5: Touched by hurtful contacts, they experience hurtful feelings that are exclusively painful—like the beings in hell.
translation_text.an4.233:2.6: These are called dark deeds with dark results.
translation_text.an4.233:3.1: And what are bright deeds with bright results?
translation_text.an4.233:3.2: It’s when someone makes pleasing choices by way of body, speech, and mind.
translation_text.an4.233:3.3: Having made these choices, they’re reborn in a pleasing world,
translation_text.an4.233:3.5: Touched by pleasing contacts, they experience pleasing feelings of perfect happiness—like the gods of universal beauty.
translation_text.an4.233:3.6: These are called bright deeds with bright results.
translation_text.an4.233:4.1: And what are dark and bright deeds with dark and bright results?
translation_text.an4.233:4.2: It’s when someone makes both hurtful and pleasing choices by way of body, speech, and mind.
translation_text.an4.233:4.3: Having made these choices, they are reborn in a world that is both hurtful and pleasing,
translation_text.an4.233:4.4: where hurtful and pleasing contacts strike them.
translation_text.an4.233:4.5: Touched by both hurtful and pleasing contacts, they experience both hurtful and pleasing feelings that are a mixture of pleasure and pain—like humans, some gods, and some beings in the underworld.
translation_text.an4.233:4.6: These are called dark and bright deeds with dark and bright results.
translation_text.an4.233:5.1: And what are neither dark nor bright deeds with neither dark nor bright results, which lead to the ending of deeds?
translation_text.an4.233:5.2: It’s the intention to give up dark deeds with dark results, bright deeds with bright results, and both dark and bright deeds with both dark and bright results.
translation_text.an4.233:5.3: These are called neither dark nor bright deeds with neither dark nor bright results, which lead to the ending of deeds.
translation_text.an4.233:5.4: These are the four kinds of deeds that I declare, having realized them with my own insight.”

variant_text.an4.233:5.2: yamidaṁ → yampidaṁ (bj, pts1ed); yampīdaṁ (sya-all, km) | yamidaṁ → yampidaṁ (bj, pts1ed); yampīdaṁ (sya-all, km)
